Title: Stronger Together: Tracking Regional Integration in the Pacific
Description: Regional Cooperation and Integration is, and will be, central to the Pacific’s development pathways. This webinar presents a customized version of the Asia Pacific Regional Cooperation and Integration Index (ARCII) tailored to Pacific developing member countries (DMCs). It assesses the subregion’s economic linkages with Asia, within its members, and with key external partners across multiple channels, including trade, investment, infrastructure and digital connectivity. The report concludes with policy recommendations to inform key regional strategies and the Pacific Roadmap for Economic Development.

Title: Asian Development Outlook April 2026 Launch
Description: The conflict in the Middle East is testing the resilience of developing Asia and the Pacific. Uncertainty remains exceptionally high, but the impacts of the conflict are already being felt—through higher energy and commodity prices, disrupted shipping routes, and tighter financial conditions. The Asian Development Outlook April 2026 examines these transmission channels and their implications for the region’s growth and inflation prospects.

Title: It is About Time: Measurement, Challenges, and Policies for Asia
Description: Time, though universal, is distributed unevenly. Time-use surveys show how people allocate hours among paid work, care, learning, leisure, and self-care, revealing gaps in productivity and opportunity. The report It Is About Time: Measurements, Challenges, and Policies for Asia reviews key challenges in survey design and presents the first harmonized analysis of microdata from eight countries. Its findings highlight the full range of paid and unpaid activities that sustain economies and underscore the need to invest in care systems, infrastructure, and labor market reforms for inclusive growth.

Title: Leveraging Regional Cooperation and Integration Amid Global Uncertainties
Description: The Asian Economic Integration Report 2026: Leveraging Regional Cooperation and Integration to Navigate Global Uncertainties examines recent progress in regional cooperation and integration by analyzing trends in cross-border trade and global value chains, foreign direct investment, finance, migration, remittances, and tourism. The webinar will share key findings of the report and discuss policy priorities—strengthening and better implementing trade agreements, leveraging digital investment, improving financial safety nets, and facilitating the movement of people.

Title: From Data to Decisions: Nature for Development
Description: Data is one of three essential pillars—alongside governance and policy—for recognizing nature as a core factor of production and scaling nature finance to support its conservation. Drawing on the Asia-Pacific Climate Report 2025: Unlocking Nature for Development, this webinar examines how high-quality environmental data strengthens economic and financial decision-making by correcting market failures and internalizing environmental externalities. The session will discuss the need for reliable, decision-grade ecological data that can be structured and made interoperable with economic statistics, supporting valuation, monitoring, and adaptive management of natural capital. It highlights how better data can inform policy, investment, and financing strategies that integrate nature into development planning.
